AIPerfLogLevel.description: Logging levels for AIPerf output verbosity.
AIPerfLogLevel.title: AIPerfLogLevel

AccuracyConfig.description:
Configuration for accuracy benchmarking mode.

When benchmark is set, enables accuracy evaluation alongside
performance profiling using standard benchmarks (MMLU, AIME, etc.).

benchmark.description: Accuracy benchmark to run (e.g., mmlu, aime, hellaswag). When set, enables accuracy benchmarking mode alongside performance profiling.

tasks.description: Specific tasks or subtasks within the benchmark to evaluate (e.g., specific MMLU subjects). If not set, all tasks are included.
tasks.title: Tasks

n_shots.description: Number of few-shot examples to include in the prompt. 0 means zero-shot evaluation. Maximum 8.
n_shots.title: N Shots

oneOf.description: Number of few-shot examples to include in the prompt. 0 means zero-shot evaluation. Maximum 8.
oneOf.title: N Shots

enable_cot.description: Enable chain-of-thought prompting for accuracy evaluation. Adds reasoning instructions to the prompt.
enable_cot.title: Enable Cot

grader.description: Override the default grader for the selected benchmark (e.g., exact_match, math, multiple_choice, code_execution). If not set, uses the benchmark's default grader.

system_prompt.description: Custom system prompt to use for accuracy evaluation. Overrides any benchmark-specific system prompt.
system_prompt.title: System Prompt

verbose.description: Enable verbose output for accuracy evaluation, showing per-problem grading details.
verbose.title: Verbose

AccuracyConfig.title: AccuracyConfig

ArtifactsConfig.description:
Artifacts configuration for benchmark output.

Controls where and how benchmark results are exported.
Uses flat structure with format arrays instead of nested export configs.

dir.description: Output directory for all benchmark artifacts. Created if it doesn't exist.
dir.title: Dir

prefix.description: Filename prefix for all exported files. Example: 'my_run' produces 'my_run_summary.json', 'my_run_records.jsonl'.
prefix.title: Prefix

summary.description: Summary export formats. Options: json, yaml. Set to false to disable.
summary.title: Summary

records.description: Per-request records export formats. Options: jsonl, csv. Set to false to disable.
records.title: Records

raw.description: Export raw request/response payloads as JSONL.
raw.title: Raw

trace.description: Export HTTP trace data for debugging.
trace.title: Trace

per_chunk_data.description: Include per-chunk list data (e.g., inter_chunk_latency arrays) in per-record exports. These arrays contain one timing value per SSE chunk and can be very large for long responses.
per_chunk_data.title: Per Chunk Data

slice_duration.description: Time slice duration in seconds for trend analysis (must be > 0). Divides benchmark into windows for per-window statistics. Supports: 30, '30s', '5m', '2h'. Accepts number (seconds) or string like '30s', '5m', '2h'.
slice_duration.title: Slice Duration

show_trace_timing.description: Display HTTP trace timing metrics in console output. Shows detailed timing breakdown: blocked, DNS, connecting, sending, waiting (TTFB), receiving, and total duration.
show_trace_timing.title: Show Trace Timing

cli_command.description: CLI command used to run the benchmark. Populated automatically when running via CLI.
cli_command.title: Cli Command

benchmark_id.description: Unique identifier for this benchmark run. Used to correlate artifacts across export formats. Auto-generated if not provided.
benchmark_id.title: Benchmark Id

ArtifactsConfig.title: ArtifactsConfig

AudioConfig.description:
Configuration for synthetic audio generation in multimodal datasets.

Controls the generation of synthetic audio for speech-to-text
and audio-language model benchmarking. Generated audio is random
noise with specified sample rate, bit depth, and format.

batch_size.description: Number of audio inputs to include in each multimodal request. Supported with chat endpoint type for multimodal models. Set to 0 to disable audio inputs.
batch_size.title: Batch Size

oneOf.description: Number of audio inputs to include in each multimodal request. Supported with chat endpoint type for multimodal models. Set to 0 to disable audio inputs.
oneOf.title: Batch Size

length.description: Audio duration in seconds. Can be a fixed value or {mean, stddev} distribution. Used when batch_size > 0 for multimodal benchmarking.
length.title: Length

format.description: File format for generated audio files. wav: uncompressed PCM (larger files). mp3: compressed (smaller files). Format affects file size in multimodal requests but not audio characteristics.

sample_rates.description: List of audio sample rates in kHz to randomly select from. Common values: 8.0 (telephony), 16.0 (speech), 44.1 (CD quality), 48.0 (professional). Specify multiple values for mixed-quality testing.
sample_rates.title: Sample Rates

depths.description: List of audio bit depths in bits to randomly select from. Each audio file is assigned a random depth from this list. Common values: 8 (low quality), 16 (CD quality), 24 (professional), 32 (high-end). Specify multiple values for mixed-quality testing.
depths.title: Depths

channels.description: Number of audio channels. 1 = mono (single channel), 2 = stereo (left/right channels). Stereo doubles file size. Most speech models use mono.
channels.title: Channels

oneOf.description: Number of audio channels. 1 = mono (single channel), 2 = stereo (left/right channels). Stereo doubles file size. Most speech models use mono.
oneOf.title: Channels

AudioConfig.title: AudioConfig

AudioFormat.description: Audio file formats for synthetic audio generation.
AudioFormat.title: AudioFormat

AugmentConfig.description:
Configuration for augmenting file datasets with output length specifications.

Used in composed datasets where file-based prompts need OSL control.

osl.description: Output sequence length to apply to augmented records. Can be a fixed integer or {mean, stddev} distribution. Behavior depends on osl_mode setting.
osl.title: Osl

osl_mode.description: How to apply OSL to records. fill: only apply if the record lacks an existing OSL value. override: always replace existing OSL.

output_distribution.description: Output length probability distribution. When specified, overrides the osl field. Each entry specifies {isl (ignored), osl, probability}. Probabilities must sum to 100.
output_distribution.title: Output Distribution

AugmentConfig.title: AugmentConfig

CancellationConfig.description:
Configuration for request cancellation testing.

Enables testing server behavior when clients cancel requests mid-flight.

rate.description: Percentage of requests to cancel (0-100). 10.0 means 10%% of requests will be cancelled.
rate.title: Rate

oneOf.description: Percentage of requests to cancel (0-100). 10.0 means 10%% of requests will be cancelled.
oneOf.title: Rate

oneOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'rate'.

delay.description: Seconds to wait after sending before cancelling. 0.0 means cancel immediately after send.
delay.title: Delay

oneOf.description: Seconds to wait after sending before cancelling. 0.0 means cancel immediately after send.
oneOf.title: Delay

CancellationConfig.title: CancellationConfig

ComposedDataset.description:
Composed dataset configuration (unique to AIPerf).

Combines file-based data with synthetic augmentation.
This enables advanced scenarios like:
- Adding system prompts to existing queries
- Testing KV cache with file-based prompts
- Adding multimodal content to text datasets
- Extending small datasets with padding

source.description: The base file dataset to augment.

augment.description: Augmentation configuration specifying prefixes, suffixes, OSL, multimodal content, and padding for the source data.

entries.description: Final dataset size after augmentation. If source has fewer entries and pad_to_count is set in augment, synthetic padding entries are generated to reach this count.
entries.title: Entries

random_seed.description: Random seed for deterministic augmentation. Overrides global random_seed for this dataset.
random_seed.title: Random Seed

ComposedDataset.title: ComposedDataset

ConcurrencyPhase.description:
Concurrency-controlled load: dispatch immediately when a slot opens.

Primary control is ``concurrency`` (defaults to 1).
No rate limiting -- pure concurrency-based throughput.

dataset.description: Name of dataset to use (from datasets section). If not specified, uses first defined dataset.
dataset.title: Dataset

exclude_from_results.description: Exclude this phase's metrics from final results. Set to true for warmup or cooldown phases.
exclude_from_results.title: Exclude From Results

anyOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'requests'.

requests.description: Stop after this many requests sent (must be >= 1).
requests.title: Requests

duration.description: Stop after this time elapsed (must be > 0). Supports: 300, '5m', '2h'. Accepts number (seconds) or string like '30s', '5m', '2h'.
duration.title: Duration

sessions.description: Stop after this many sessions completed (must be >= 1).
sessions.title: Sessions

concurrency.description: Max concurrent in-flight requests (must be >= 1). Primary control for concurrency phases.
concurrency.title: Concurrency

oneOf.description: Max concurrent in-flight requests (must be >= 1). Primary control for concurrency phases.
oneOf.title: Concurrency

oneOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'concurrency'.

concurrency_ramp.description: Ramp concurrency from lower value. Can be number (seconds) or {duration, strategy}.

prefill_concurrency.description: Max concurrent requests in prefill stage (must be >= 1). Limits requests before first token received.
prefill_concurrency.title: Prefill Concurrency

prefill_ramp.description: Ramp prefill_concurrency from lower value. Can be number (seconds) or {duration, strategy}.

grace_period.description: Seconds to wait for in-flight requests after duration expires (must be >= 0). Requires 'duration' to be set. Supports: 30, '30s', '2m'. Accepts number (seconds) or string like '30s', '5m', '2h'.
grace_period.title: Grace Period

seamless.description: Start this phase immediately when previous phase stops, without waiting for in-flight requests to complete. Cannot be True for the first phase.
seamless.title: Seamless

ConcurrencyPhase.title: ConcurrencyPhase

ConnectionReuseStrategy.description: Transport connection reuse strategy. Controls how and when connections are reused across requests.
ConnectionReuseStrategy.title: ConnectionReuseStrategy

dataset.description: Name of dataset to use (from datasets section). If not specified, uses first defined dataset.
dataset.title: Dataset

exclude_from_results.description: Exclude this phase's metrics from final results. Set to true for warmup or cooldown phases.
exclude_from_results.title: Exclude From Results

anyOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'requests'.

requests.description: Stop after this many requests sent (must be >= 1).
requests.title: Requests

duration.description: Stop after this time elapsed (must be > 0). Supports: 300, '5m', '2h'. Accepts number (seconds) or string like '30s', '5m', '2h'.
duration.title: Duration

sessions.description: Stop after this many sessions completed (must be >= 1).
sessions.title: Sessions

anyOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'concurrency'.

concurrency.description: Max concurrent in-flight requests (must be >= 1). For concurrency type: primary control. For rate types: acts as a cap.
concurrency.title: Concurrency

concurrency_ramp.description: Ramp concurrency from lower value. Can be number (seconds) or {duration, strategy}.

prefill_concurrency.description: Max concurrent requests in prefill stage (must be >= 1). Limits requests before first token received.
prefill_concurrency.title: Prefill Concurrency

prefill_ramp.description: Ramp prefill_concurrency from lower value. Can be number (seconds) or {duration, strategy}.

grace_period.description: Seconds to wait for in-flight requests after duration expires (must be >= 0). Requires 'duration' to be set. Supports: 30, '30s', '2m'. Accepts number (seconds) or string like '30s', '5m', '2h'.
grace_period.title: Grace Period

seamless.description: Start this phase immediately when previous phase stops, without waiting for in-flight requests to complete. Cannot be True for the first phase.
seamless.title: Seamless

rate.description: Target request rate in requests per second (must be > 0).
rate.title: Rate

oneOf.description: Target request rate in requests per second (must be > 0).
oneOf.title: Rate

oneOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'rate'.

rate_ramp.description: Ramp rate from lower value. Can be number (seconds) or {duration, strategy}.

DatasetFormat.description: Defines the format of file-based datasets.
DatasetFormat.title: DatasetFormat

DualBindCommunicationConfig.description:
Dual-bind (IPC + TCP) communication configuration.

For Kubernetes deployments where controller services connect via IPC
(co-located in same pod) and worker pods connect via TCP.

When controller_host is None, services use IPC (local mode).
When controller_host is set, services use TCP to that host (remote mode).

ipc_path.description: Directory for IPC socket files (local services).
ipc_path.title: Ipc Path

tcp_host.description: TCP bind host for proxies. Use 0.0.0.0 to listen on all interfaces.
tcp_host.title: Tcp Host

controller_host.description: Controller host for remote TCP connections. When set, services connect via TCP to this host instead of IPC. In Kubernetes, set via JobSet DNS (e.g., controller.namespace.svc).
controller_host.title: Controller Host

DualBindCommunicationConfig.title: DualBindCommunicationConfig

points.description: Weighted discrete values. Weights are relative.
points.title: Points

EmpiricalDistribution.title: EmpiricalDistribution

EmpiricalPoint.description: A weighted value in an empirical distribution.
EmpiricalPoint.title: EmpiricalPoint

EndpointConfig.description:
Endpoint configuration for connecting to inference servers.

This section configures how AIPerf connects to and communicates
with the target inference server(s). It supports multiple URLs
for load-balanced deployments and various API types.

urls.description: List of server URLs to benchmark. Requests distributed according to url_strategy. Example: ['http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions']
urls.title: Urls

url_strategy.description: Strategy for distributing requests across multiple URLs. round_robin cycles through URLs in order.

type.description: API endpoint type determining request/response format. chat: OpenAI chat completions, completions: OpenAI completions, embeddings: vector embeddings, rankings: reranking, template: custom format.

path.description: Override default endpoint path. Use for servers with non-standard API paths. Example: '/custom/v2/generate'
path.title: Path

api_key.description: API authentication key. Supports environment variable substitution: ${OPENAI_API_KEY}. Can also use ${VAR:default} syntax for defaults.
api_key.title: Api Key

timeout.description: Request timeout in seconds (0 = no timeout). Requests exceeding this duration are marked as failed. Should exceed expected max response time.
timeout.title: Timeout

oneOf.description: Request timeout in seconds (0 = no timeout). Requests exceeding this duration are marked as failed. Should exceed expected max response time.
oneOf.title: Timeout

ready_check_timeout.description: Seconds to wait for endpoint readiness before benchmarking (0 = skip). Sends a real inference request to verify the model is loaded and can generate output, not just a /health check.
ready_check_timeout.title: Ready Check Timeout

oneOf.description: Seconds to wait for endpoint readiness before benchmarking (0 = skip). Sends a real inference request to verify the model is loaded and can generate output, not just a /health check.
oneOf.title: Ready Check Timeout

streaming.description: Enable streaming (Server-Sent Events) responses. Required for accurate TTFT (time to first token) measurement. Server must support streaming for this to work.
streaming.title: Streaming

transport.description: HTTP transport protocol (http/https). Auto-detected from URL scheme if not specified. Explicit setting overrides auto-detection.

connection_reuse.description: HTTP connection management strategy. pooled: shared connection pool (fastest), never: new connection per request (includes TCP overhead), sticky_sessions: dedicated connection per session.

use_legacy_max_tokens.description: Use 'max_tokens' field instead of 'max_completion_tokens'. Enable for compatibility with older OpenAI API versions.
use_legacy_max_tokens.title: Use Legacy Max Tokens

use_server_token_count.description: Use server-reported token counts from response usage field. When true, trusts usage.prompt_tokens and usage.completion_tokens. When false, counts tokens locally using configured tokenizer.
use_server_token_count.title: Use Server Token Count

template.description: Custom template configuration for template endpoint type. Only used when type='template'. Defines request body format and response parsing.

headers.description: Custom HTTP headers to include in all requests. Useful for authentication, tracing, or routing. Values support environment variable substitution.
headers.title: Headers

extra.description: Additional fields to include in request body. Merged into every request. Common fields: temperature, top_p, top_k, stop.
extra.title: Extra

download_video_content.description: For video generation endpoints, download the video content after generation completes. Adds a content download step to the async polling flow.
download_video_content.title: Download Video Content

url.description: Shorthand for 'urls'. Accepts a single value which will be converted to a list/dict. List of server URLs to benchmark. Requests distributed according to url_strategy. Example: ['http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions']

oneOf.description: List of server URLs to benchmark. Requests distributed according to url_strategy. Example: ['http://localhost:8000/v1/chat/completions']
oneOf.title: Urls

EndpointConfig.title: EndpointConfig

FileDataset.description:
File-based dataset configuration.

Loads prompts from a local file in various formats.
Supports trace replay and custom sampling strategies.

path.description: Path to file or directory containing benchmark dataset. Can be absolute or relative. Supported formats depend on the format field: JSONL for single_turn/multi_turn, JSONL trace files for mooncake_trace, directories for random_pool.
path.title: Path

format.description: Dataset file format determining parsing logic and expected file structure. single_turn: JSONL with single prompt-response exchanges. multi_turn: JSONL with conversation history. mooncake_trace: timestamped trace files for replay. random_pool: directory of reusable prompts.

sampling.description: Strategy for selecting entries from dataset during benchmarking. sequential: iterate in order, wrapping to start after end. random: randomly sample with replacement (entries may repeat). shuffle: random permutation without replacement, re-shuffling after exhaustion.

synthesis.description: Trace synthesis/transformation configuration. Allows scaling timestamps and token lengths before replay. Only used with mooncake_trace format.

entries.description: Limit number of records to use from file. If not specified, uses all records in the file.
entries.title: Entries

random_seed.description: Random seed for deterministic sampling. When set, makes random/shuffle sampling reproducible across runs. Overrides global random_seed for this dataset.
random_seed.title: Random Seed

FileDataset.title: FileDataset

FileSourceConfig.description:
File source configuration for composed datasets.

Simplified file dataset specification used within composed
dataset source field.

type.description: Source type. Must be 'file' for composed datasets.
type.title: Type

path.description: Path to the source file. Can be absolute or relative.
path.title: Path

format.description: Dataset file format determining parsing logic. single_turn: JSONL with single exchanges. multi_turn: JSONL with conversation history. mooncake_trace: timestamped trace files.

sampling.description: Strategy for selecting entries from the source file. sequential: iterate in order. random: randomly sample with replacement. shuffle: random permutation without replacement.

FileSourceConfig.title: FileSourceConfig

FixedDistribution.title: FixedDistribution
FixedDistribution.description: Returns a constant value on every sample. Scalars coerce to this. Can be a number (interpreted as fixed value) or a distribution object with type field.

anyOf.description: Returns a constant value on every sample. Scalars coerce to this.

value.description: The constant value returned on every sample.
value.title: Value

anyOf.title: FixedDistribution

FixedSchedulePhase.description:
Replay requests at predetermined timestamps from a trace dataset.

Stop condition not required -- the trace dataset determines when the
phase ends.

dataset.description: Name of dataset to use (from datasets section). If not specified, uses first defined dataset.
dataset.title: Dataset

exclude_from_results.description: Exclude this phase's metrics from final results. Set to true for warmup or cooldown phases.
exclude_from_results.title: Exclude From Results

anyOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'requests'.

requests.description: Stop after this many requests sent (must be >= 1).
requests.title: Requests

duration.description: Stop after this time elapsed (must be > 0). Supports: 300, '5m', '2h'. Accepts number (seconds) or string like '30s', '5m', '2h'.
duration.title: Duration

sessions.description: Stop after this many sessions completed (must be >= 1).
sessions.title: Sessions

anyOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'concurrency'.

concurrency.description: Max concurrent in-flight requests (must be >= 1). For concurrency type: primary control. For rate types: acts as a cap.
concurrency.title: Concurrency

concurrency_ramp.description: Ramp concurrency from lower value. Can be number (seconds) or {duration, strategy}.

prefill_concurrency.description: Max concurrent requests in prefill stage (must be >= 1). Limits requests before first token received.
prefill_concurrency.title: Prefill Concurrency

prefill_ramp.description: Ramp prefill_concurrency from lower value. Can be number (seconds) or {duration, strategy}.

grace_period.description: Seconds to wait for in-flight requests after duration expires (must be >= 0). Requires 'duration' to be set. Supports: 30, '30s', '2m'. Accepts number (seconds) or string like '30s', '5m', '2h'.
grace_period.title: Grace Period

seamless.description: Start this phase immediately when previous phase stops, without waiting for in-flight requests to complete. Cannot be True for the first phase.
seamless.title: Seamless

auto_offset.description: Normalize trace timestamps to start at 0. Subtracts minimum timestamp from all entries.
auto_offset.title: Auto Offset

start_offset.description: Filter out trace requests before this timestamp in ms (must be >= 0).
start_offset.title: Start Offset

end_offset.description: Filter out trace requests after this timestamp in ms (must be >= 0).
end_offset.title: End Offset

FixedSchedulePhase.title: FixedSchedulePhase

GammaPhase.description: Gamma-distributed request arrivals with configurable smoothness.

dataset.description: Name of dataset to use (from datasets section). If not specified, uses first defined dataset.
dataset.title: Dataset

exclude_from_results.description: Exclude this phase's metrics from final results. Set to true for warmup or cooldown phases.
exclude_from_results.title: Exclude From Results

anyOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'requests'.

requests.description: Stop after this many requests sent (must be >= 1).
requests.title: Requests

duration.description: Stop after this time elapsed (must be > 0). Supports: 300, '5m', '2h'. Accepts number (seconds) or string like '30s', '5m', '2h'.
duration.title: Duration

sessions.description: Stop after this many sessions completed (must be >= 1).
sessions.title: Sessions

anyOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'concurrency'.

concurrency.description: Max concurrent in-flight requests (must be >= 1). For concurrency type: primary control. For rate types: acts as a cap.
concurrency.title: Concurrency

concurrency_ramp.description: Ramp concurrency from lower value. Can be number (seconds) or {duration, strategy}.

prefill_concurrency.description: Max concurrent requests in prefill stage (must be >= 1). Limits requests before first token received.
prefill_concurrency.title: Prefill Concurrency

prefill_ramp.description: Ramp prefill_concurrency from lower value. Can be number (seconds) or {duration, strategy}.

grace_period.description: Seconds to wait for in-flight requests after duration expires (must be >= 0). Requires 'duration' to be set. Supports: 30, '30s', '2m'. Accepts number (seconds) or string like '30s', '5m', '2h'.
grace_period.title: Grace Period

seamless.description: Start this phase immediately when previous phase stops, without waiting for in-flight requests to complete. Cannot be True for the first phase.
seamless.title: Seamless

rate.description: Target request rate in requests per second (must be > 0).
rate.title: Rate

oneOf.description: Target request rate in requests per second (must be > 0).
oneOf.title: Rate

oneOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'rate'.

rate_ramp.description: Ramp rate from lower value. Can be number (seconds) or {duration, strategy}.

smoothness.description: Gamma distribution shape parameter (must be > 0). 1.0 = Poisson, <1 = bursty, >1 = regular.
smoothness.title: Smoothness

GammaPhase.title: GammaPhase

GpuTelemetryConfig.title: GpuTelemetryConfig
GpuTelemetryConfig.description:
GPU telemetry configuration for DCGM metrics collection.

Collects GPU metrics (power, utilization, temperature, memory usage)
from NVIDIA DCGM exporter endpoints.

Accepts shorthand forms:
    - String URL: "http://localhost:9400/metrics"
      → GpuTelemetryConfig(enabled=True, urls=["http://localhost:9400/metrics"])
    - Singular url field: {url: "..."}
      → GpuTelemetryConfig(urls=["..."]) Can be a URL string or full config object.

anyOf.description:
GPU telemetry configuration for DCGM metrics collection.

Collects GPU metrics (power, utilization, temperature, memory usage)
from NVIDIA DCGM exporter endpoints.

Accepts shorthand forms:
    - String URL: "http://localhost:9400/metrics"
      → GpuTelemetryConfig(enabled=True, urls=["http://localhost:9400/metrics"])
    - Singular url field: {url: "..."}
      → GpuTelemetryConfig(urls=["..."])

enabled.description: Enable GPU telemetry collection. Set to false to disable.
enabled.title: Enabled

urls.description: DCGM exporter endpoint URLs. Example: http://localhost:9400/metrics
urls.title: Urls

metrics_file.description: Path to CSV file with pre-recorded GPU metrics. Alternative to live DCGM collection.
metrics_file.title: Metrics File

url.description: Shorthand for 'urls'. Accepts a single value which will be converted to a list/dict. DCGM exporter endpoint URLs. Example: http://localhost:9400/metrics

oneOf.description: DCGM exporter endpoint URLs. Example: http://localhost:9400/metrics
oneOf.title: Urls

anyOf.title: GpuTelemetryConfig

GridSweep.description: Grid sweep - all combinations of parameters (Cartesian product).

variables.description: Variables to sweep: dot-notation path -> list of values.
variables.title: Variables

GridSweep.title: GridSweep

ImageConfig.description:
Configuration for synthetic image generation in multimodal datasets.

Controls the generation of synthetic images for vision-language
model benchmarking. Images are generated by randomly sampling and
resizing source images to specified dimensions.

batch_size.description: Number of images to include in each multimodal request. Supported with chat endpoint type for vision-language models. Set to 0 to disable image inputs. Higher batch sizes test multi-image understanding and increase request payload size.
batch_size.title: Batch Size

oneOf.description: Number of images to include in each multimodal request. Supported with chat endpoint type for vision-language models. Set to 0 to disable image inputs. Higher batch sizes test multi-image understanding and increase request payload size.
oneOf.title: Batch Size

width.description: Image width in pixels. Can be a fixed integer or {mean, stddev} distribution. Combined with height to determine image dimensions and file sizes for multimodal benchmarking.
width.title: Width

height.description: Image height in pixels. Can be a fixed integer or {mean, stddev} distribution. Used when batch_size > 0 for multimodal vision benchmarking.
height.title: Height

format.description: Image file format for generated images. png: lossless compression (larger files, best quality). jpeg: lossy compression (smaller files, good quality). random: randomly select between PNG and JPEG per image. Format affects file size in multimodal requests and encoding overhead.

ImageConfig.title: ImageConfig

ImageFormat.description: Image file formats for synthetic image generation.
ImageFormat.title: ImageFormat

IpcCommunicationConfig.description:
IPC (Unix socket) communication configuration.

For single-machine deployments with lowest latency.
Uses Unix domain sockets for all inter-service communication.

path.description: Directory for IPC socket files. AIPerf creates multiple socket files in this directory.
path.title: Path

IpcCommunicationConfig.title: IpcCommunicationConfig

LogNormalDistribution.description:
Log-normal parameterized by mean and median (right-skewed, always positive).

Skew is controlled by the mean/median ratio: larger ratio = heavier right tail.
When mean == median the distribution is deterministic.

Internally: sigma = sqrt(2 * log(mean / median)), mu = log(median).

mean.description: Desired mean of the output distribution.
mean.title: Mean

oneOf.description: Desired mean of the output distribution.
oneOf.title: Mean

oneOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'mean'.

median.description: Desired median. Must be <= mean. Lower median = more right skew.
median.title: Median

oneOf.description: Desired median. Must be <= mean. Lower median = more right skew.
oneOf.title: Median

LogNormalDistribution.title: LogNormalDistribution

LoggingConfig.description: Logging configuration for verbosity and debug settings.

level.description: Global logging verbosity level. trace: most verbose, error: least verbose.

LoggingConfig.title: LoggingConfig

ModelItem.description:
Configuration for a single model in advanced models configuration.

Used when the models section uses the advanced format with
explicit items, weights, and per-model settings.

name.description: Model name or identifier as known to the inference server.
name.title: Name

weight.description: Selection weight for weighted strategy (0.0-1.0). Weights are normalized across all models. Example: weight=0.7 means ~70%% of requests to this model.
weight.title: Weight

lora.description: LoRA adapter name to load with this model. Server must support dynamic LoRA adapter loading.
lora.title: Lora

modalities.description: List of input modalities this model supports. Used with modality_aware selection strategy. Valid values: 'text', 'image', 'audio', 'video'.
modalities.title: Modalities

tokenizer.description: Per-model tokenizer override. Use when this model requires a different tokenizer than global config.

ModelItem.title: ModelItem

ModelSelectionStrategy.description: Strategy for selecting the model to use for the request.
ModelSelectionStrategy.title: ModelSelectionStrategy

ModelsAdvanced.description:
Advanced models configuration with selection strategy and item details.

Use this format when you need weighted routing, LoRA adapters,
modality-aware selection, or per-model tokenizer overrides.

strategy.description: Strategy for selecting models when multiple are configured. round_robin cycles through models, random selects randomly, weighted uses configured weights, modality_aware routes by input type.

items.description: List of model configurations. At least one model required.
items.title: Items

ModelsAdvanced.title: ModelsAdvanced

MultiRunConfig.description:
Configuration for multi-run benchmarking with statistical reporting.

When num_runs > 1, AIPerf executes multiple benchmark runs and computes
aggregate statistics (mean, std, confidence intervals) across runs.

num_runs.description: Number of profile runs to execute for confidence reporting. When 1, runs a single benchmark. When >1, computes aggregate statistics across runs.
num_runs.title: Num Runs

oneOf.description: Number of profile runs to execute for confidence reporting. When 1, runs a single benchmark. When >1, computes aggregate statistics across runs.
oneOf.title: Num Runs

cooldown_seconds.description: Cooldown duration in seconds between profile runs. Allows the system to stabilize between runs.
cooldown_seconds.title: Cooldown Seconds

oneOf.description: Cooldown duration in seconds between profile runs. Allows the system to stabilize between runs.
oneOf.title: Cooldown Seconds

confidence_level.description: Confidence level for computing confidence intervals (0-1). Common values: 0.90 (90%%), 0.95 (95%%), 0.99 (99%%).
confidence_level.title: Confidence Level

oneOf.description: Confidence level for computing confidence intervals (0-1). Common values: 0.90 (90%%), 0.95 (95%%), 0.99 (99%%).
oneOf.title: Confidence Level

set_consistent_seed.description: Auto-set random seed if not specified for workload consistency.
set_consistent_seed.title: Set Consistent Seed

disable_warmup_after_first.description: Disable warmup for runs after the first. When true, only the first run includes warmup for steady-state measurement.
disable_warmup_after_first.title: Disable Warmup After First

MultiRunConfig.title: MultiRunConfig

MultimodalDistribution.title: MultimodalDistribution

NormalDistribution.description: Gaussian (truncated at 0) parameterized by mean and stddev.

oneOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'mean'.

NormalDistribution.title: NormalDistribution

OslMode.description: Defines how output sequence length is handled in composed datasets.
OslMode.title: OslMode

PeakEntry.description:
A weighted component in a multimodal distribution.

The weight and distribution fields are written inline in YAML:
    {mean: 128, stddev: 20, weight: 60}

The ``weight`` key is extracted before the remaining fields are parsed
as a SamplingDistribution. Defaults to 1.0 (equal split when omitted).
PeakEntry.title: PeakEntry

PoissonPhase.description: Poisson-distributed request arrivals at the target rate.

dataset.description: Name of dataset to use (from datasets section). If not specified, uses first defined dataset.
dataset.title: Dataset

exclude_from_results.description: Exclude this phase's metrics from final results. Set to true for warmup or cooldown phases.
exclude_from_results.title: Exclude From Results

anyOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'requests'.

requests.description: Stop after this many requests sent (must be >= 1).
requests.title: Requests

duration.description: Stop after this time elapsed (must be > 0). Supports: 300, '5m', '2h'. Accepts number (seconds) or string like '30s', '5m', '2h'.
duration.title: Duration

sessions.description: Stop after this many sessions completed (must be >= 1).
sessions.title: Sessions

anyOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'concurrency'.

concurrency.description: Max concurrent in-flight requests (must be >= 1). For concurrency type: primary control. For rate types: acts as a cap.
concurrency.title: Concurrency

concurrency_ramp.description: Ramp concurrency from lower value. Can be number (seconds) or {duration, strategy}.

prefill_concurrency.description: Max concurrent requests in prefill stage (must be >= 1). Limits requests before first token received.
prefill_concurrency.title: Prefill Concurrency

prefill_ramp.description: Ramp prefill_concurrency from lower value. Can be number (seconds) or {duration, strategy}.

grace_period.description: Seconds to wait for in-flight requests after duration expires (must be >= 0). Requires 'duration' to be set. Supports: 30, '30s', '2m'. Accepts number (seconds) or string like '30s', '5m', '2h'.
grace_period.title: Grace Period

seamless.description: Start this phase immediately when previous phase stops, without waiting for in-flight requests to complete. Cannot be True for the first phase.
seamless.title: Seamless

rate.description: Target request rate in requests per second (must be > 0).
rate.title: Rate

oneOf.description: Target request rate in requests per second (must be > 0).
oneOf.title: Rate

oneOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'rate'.

rate_ramp.description: Ramp rate from lower value. Can be number (seconds) or {duration, strategy}.

PoissonPhase.title: PoissonPhase

PrefixPromptConfig.description:
Configuration for prefix prompts (KV cache testing).

Prefix prompts allow testing KV cache efficiency by generating
requests that share common prefixes. This simulates scenarios
like system prompts or shared context that can be cached.

Note: pool_size/length are mutually exclusive with shared_system_length
and user_context_length.

pool_size.description: Number of distinct prefix prompts to generate for KV cache testing. Each prefix is prepended to user prompts, simulating cached context scenarios. Prefixes are randomly selected from pool per request. Mutually exclusive with shared_system_length/user_context_length.
pool_size.title: Pool Size

length.description: Token length for each prefix prompt in the pool. Only used when pool_size is set. Note: due to prefix and user prompts being concatenated, the final prompt token count may be off by one. Mutually exclusive with shared_system_length/user_context_length.
length.title: Length

shared_system_length.description: Length of shared system prompt in tokens. This prompt is identical across all sessions and appears as a system message. First part of a two-part prefix structure with high cache hit rate expected. Mutually exclusive with pool_size/length.
shared_system_length.title: Shared System Length

user_context_length.description: Length of per-session user context prompt in tokens. Each dataset entry gets a unique user context prompt. Second part of two-part prefix structure with lower cache hit rate expected. Mutually exclusive with pool_size/length.
user_context_length.title: User Context Length

PrefixPromptConfig.title: PrefixPromptConfig

PromptConfig.description:
Configuration for prompt/token specifications in synthetic datasets.

This is the core configuration for controlling input sequence length (ISL)
and output sequence length (OSL) in synthetic data generation.

isl.description: Input sequence length in tokens. Can be a fixed integer (e.g., 512) or distribution {mean: 512, stddev: 50}. AIPerf generates prompts with lengths following a normal distribution around the mean (±stddev). Ignored when sequence_distribution is specified.
isl.title: Isl

osl.description: Output sequence length (max tokens to request via max_completion_tokens). Can be a fixed integer or distribution {mean, stddev}. Controls response length for synthetic datasets. When not set, the model determines output length. Ignored when sequence_distribution is specified.
osl.title: Osl

block_size.description: Token block size for hash-based prompt caching in mooncake_trace datasets. When hash_ids are provided in trace entries, prompts are divided into blocks of this size. Each hash_id maps to a cached block, enabling simulation of KV-cache sharing patterns from production workloads. Total prompt length = (num_hash_ids - 1) * block_size + final_block_size.
block_size.title: Block Size

batch_size.description: Number of text inputs to include in each request for batch processing endpoints. Supported by embeddings and rankings endpoint types where models can process multiple inputs simultaneously. Set to 1 for single-input requests. Not applicable to chat or completions endpoints.
batch_size.title: Batch Size

oneOf.description: Number of text inputs to include in each request for batch processing endpoints. Supported by embeddings and rankings endpoint types where models can process multiple inputs simultaneously. Set to 1 for single-input requests. Not applicable to chat or completions endpoints.
oneOf.title: Batch Size

sequence_distribution.description: Distribution of (ISL, OSL) pairs with probabilities for mixed workload simulation. Each entry specifies {isl, osl, probability}. Probabilities are percentages (0-100) and must sum to 100. When specified, requests are sampled from this distribution instead of using isl/osl fields.
sequence_distribution.title: Sequence Distribution

PromptConfig.title: PromptConfig

PublicDataset.description:
Public dataset configuration.

Uses well-known public benchmarking datasets that are
automatically downloaded and processed by AIPerf.

name.description: Pre-configured public dataset to download and use for benchmarking. AIPerf automatically downloads and parses these datasets.

entries.description: Limit number of records to use from the dataset. If not specified, uses all available records.
entries.title: Entries

random_seed.description: Random seed for deterministic sampling from the dataset. Overrides global random_seed for this dataset.
random_seed.title: Random Seed

sampling.description: Strategy for selecting entries from dataset during benchmarking. sequential: iterate in order, wrapping to start after end. random: randomly sample with replacement (entries may repeat). shuffle: random permutation without replacement, re-shuffling after exhaustion.

PublicDataset.title: PublicDataset

RampConfig.title: RampConfig
RampConfig.description:
Configuration for gradual value ramping.

Controls how a value (concurrency, rate, etc.) transitions from
start to target over time. Can be a duration (number in seconds or string like '30s', '5m', '2h') or {duration, strategy} object.

anyOf.description:
Configuration for gradual value ramping.

Controls how a value (concurrency, rate, etc.) transitions from
start to target over time.

duration.description: Seconds to ramp from start to target value.
duration.title: Duration

strategy.description: Ramp curve shape: linear (constant rate), exponential (slow start, fast finish), poisson (stochastic with guaranteed completion).

anyOf.title: RampConfig

RankingsConfig.description:
Configuration for rankings/reranking endpoint datasets.

Controls the generation of query-passage pairs for benchmarking
reranking and ranking models. Each request contains one query
and multiple passages to rank.

passages.description: Number of passages per ranking request. Can be a fixed integer or {mean, stddev} distribution. Higher values test ranking at scale but increase request payload size and processing time.
passages.title: Passages

passage_tokens.description: Token length for each passage in ranking requests. Can be a fixed integer or {mean, stddev} distribution. Passages are synthetically generated text. Longer passages increase input processing demands and request size.
passage_tokens.title: Passage Tokens

query_tokens.description: Token length for the query text in ranking requests. Can be a fixed integer or {mean, stddev} distribution. Each ranking request contains one query and multiple passages.
query_tokens.title: Query Tokens

RankingsConfig.title: RankingsConfig

ui.description: User interface mode. dashboard: rich interactive UI, simple: text progress, none: silent operation.

workers.description: Maximum worker processes. null = auto-detect based on CPU cores.
workers.title: Workers

record_processors.description: Number of parallel record processors. null = auto-detect based on CPU cores.
record_processors.title: Record Processors

service_run_type.description: Type of service run. multiprocessing: local multi-process (default), kubernetes: distributed across pods.

communication.description: Inter-process communication configuration. Defaults to IPC for single-machine operation.
communication.title: Communication

api_port.description: AIPerf API server port. Enables HTTP and WebSocket endpoints for real-time metrics and control.
api_port.title: Api Port

api_host.description: AIPerf API server host. Requires api_port to be set.
api_host.title: Api Host

dataset_api_base_url.description: Base URL for dataset API endpoints in Kubernetes mode. Set by the runner to allow worker pods to download datasets.
dataset_api_base_url.title: Dataset Api Base Url

workers_per_pod.description: Number of worker subprocesses per Kubernetes worker pod.
workers_per_pod.title: Workers Per Pod

record_processors_per_pod.description: Number of record processor subprocesses per Kubernetes worker pod.
record_processors_per_pod.title: Record Processors Per Pod

ScenarioSweep.description: Scenario sweep - hand-picked configurations deep-merged with base.

runs.description: List of scenario dicts to deep-merge with base config.
runs.title: Runs

ScenarioSweep.title: ScenarioSweep

isl.description: Input sequence length (tokens). Can be a fixed integer, a {mean, stddev} distribution, or any distribution like {mean: 512, median: 400} for lognormal.
isl.title: Isl

isl_stddev.description: Shorthand for ISL standard deviation. If provided when isl is an integer, creates a normal distribution. Cannot be used when isl is already a distribution dict.
isl_stddev.title: Isl Stddev

osl.description: Output sequence length (tokens). Can be a fixed integer, a {mean, stddev} distribution, or any typed distribution.
osl.title: Osl

osl_stddev.description: Shorthand for OSL standard deviation. If provided when osl is an integer, creates a normal distribution. Cannot be used when osl is already a distribution dict.
osl_stddev.title: Osl Stddev

probability.description: Relative probability weight for this distribution bucket (0-100). Weights are normalized across all entries. Example: probability=40 means 40%% of requests use this ISL/OSL.
probability.title: Probability

oneOf.description: Relative probability weight for this distribution bucket (0-100). Weights are normalized across all entries. Example: probability=40 means 40%% of requests use this ISL/OSL.
oneOf.title: Probability

SequenceDistributionEntry.title: SequenceDistributionEntry

ServerMetricsConfig.title: ServerMetricsConfig
ServerMetricsConfig.description:
Server metrics configuration for Prometheus scraping.

Collects server-side operational metrics (queue depth, KV cache utilization,
batch sizes, GPU memory) from Prometheus endpoints exposed by inference servers
like vLLM, TensorRT-LLM, or Triton.

Accepts shorthand forms:
    - String URL: "http://localhost:9090/metrics"
      → ServerMetricsConfig(enabled=True, urls=["http://localhost:9090/metrics"])
    - Singular url field: {url: "..."}
      → ServerMetricsConfig(urls=["..."]) Can be a URL string or full config object.

anyOf.description:
Server metrics configuration for Prometheus scraping.

Collects server-side operational metrics (queue depth, KV cache utilization,
batch sizes, GPU memory) from Prometheus endpoints exposed by inference servers
like vLLM, TensorRT-LLM, or Triton.

Accepts shorthand forms:
    - String URL: "http://localhost:9090/metrics"
      → ServerMetricsConfig(enabled=True, urls=["http://localhost:9090/metrics"])
    - Singular url field: {url: "..."}
      → ServerMetricsConfig(urls=["..."])

enabled.description: Enable Prometheus metrics scraping. Set to false to disable.
enabled.title: Enabled

urls.description: Prometheus metrics endpoint URLs to scrape. Typically the /metrics endpoint on inference servers.
urls.title: Urls

formats.description: Export formats for scraped metrics. Options: json, csv, parquet, jsonl.
formats.title: Formats

discovery.description: Auto-discovery of Prometheus endpoints in Kubernetes. Discovers pods via Dynamo labels, Prometheus annotations, or custom label selectors.

url.description: Shorthand for 'urls'. Accepts a single value which will be converted to a list/dict. Prometheus metrics endpoint URLs to scrape. Typically the /metrics endpoint on inference servers.

oneOf.description: Prometheus metrics endpoint URLs to scrape. Typically the /metrics endpoint on inference servers.
oneOf.title: Urls

anyOf.title: ServerMetricsConfig

ServerMetricsDiscoveryConfig.description:
Kubernetes-based auto-discovery of Prometheus /metrics endpoints.

When mode is 'auto' or 'kubernetes', queries the K8s API for pods with:
1. Dynamo label: nvidia.com/metrics-enabled=true
2. Prometheus annotation: prometheus.io/scrape=true
3. User-provided label_selector (server-side filter)

Prometheus annotations (prometheus.io/port, prometheus.io/path,
prometheus.io/scheme) control the constructed scrape URL when present.

mode.description: Discovery mode: 'auto' detects environment and tries K8s if in-cluster, 'kubernetes' forces K8s API discovery, 'disabled' uses only explicit URLs.

label_selector.description: Kubernetes label selector for discovery. Example: 'app=vllm,env=prod'. Applied in addition to built-in Dynamo and Prometheus discovery.
label_selector.title: Label Selector

namespace.description: Kubernetes namespace to search. If not specified, searches all namespaces.
namespace.title: Namespace

ServerMetricsDiscoveryConfig.title: ServerMetricsDiscoveryConfig

ServerMetricsDiscoveryMode.description:
Mode for discovering server metrics endpoints in distributed environments.

Controls how AIPerf discovers Prometheus /metrics endpoints for server metrics collection.
ServerMetricsDiscoveryMode.title: ServerMetricsDiscoveryMode

ServerMetricsFormat.description:
Format options for server metrics export.

Controls which output files are generated for server metrics data.
Default selection is JSON + CSV (JSONL excluded to avoid large files).
ServerMetricsFormat.title: ServerMetricsFormat

SynthesisConfig.description:
Configuration for trace synthesis/transformation.

Used with mooncake_trace format to transform production trace
data before replay. Allows scaling timestamps, token lengths,
and radix tree structure.

speedup_ratio.description: Multiplier for timestamp scaling in synthesized traces. 1.0 = real-time, 2.0 = 2x faster, 0.5 = 2x slower.
speedup_ratio.title: Speedup Ratio

oneOf.description: Multiplier for timestamp scaling in synthesized traces. 1.0 = real-time, 2.0 = 2x faster, 0.5 = 2x slower.
oneOf.title: Speedup Ratio

prefix_len_multiplier.description: Multiplier for core prefix branch lengths in the radix tree. 1.5 means prefix branches are 50%% longer.
prefix_len_multiplier.title: Prefix Len Multiplier

oneOf.description: Multiplier for core prefix branch lengths in the radix tree. 1.5 means prefix branches are 50%% longer.
oneOf.title: Prefix Len Multiplier

prefix_root_multiplier.description: Number of independent radix trees to distribute traces across. Higher values increase prefix diversity.
prefix_root_multiplier.title: Prefix Root Multiplier

oneOf.description: Number of independent radix trees to distribute traces across. Higher values increase prefix diversity.
oneOf.title: Prefix Root Multiplier

prompt_len_multiplier.description: Multiplier for leaf path (unique prompt) lengths. 2.0 means prompts are 2x longer.
prompt_len_multiplier.title: Prompt Len Multiplier

oneOf.description: Multiplier for leaf path (unique prompt) lengths. 2.0 means prompts are 2x longer.
oneOf.title: Prompt Len Multiplier

max_isl.description: Maximum input sequence length filter. Traces with input_length > max_isl are skipped entirely.
max_isl.title: Max Isl

max_osl.description: Maximum output sequence length cap. Traces with output_length > max_osl are capped to this value (not filtered).
max_osl.title: Max Osl

SynthesisConfig.title: SynthesisConfig

SyntheticDataset.description:
Synthetic dataset configuration.

Generates prompts programmatically based on token length
specifications. Ideal for controlled experiments.

entries.description: Total number of unique entries to generate for the dataset. Each entry represents a unique prompt with sampled ISL/OSL. Entries are reused across conversations and turns according to the sampling strategy. Higher values provide more diversity.
entries.title: Entries

oneOf.description: Total number of unique entries to generate for the dataset. Each entry represents a unique prompt with sampled ISL/OSL. Entries are reused across conversations and turns according to the sampling strategy. Higher values provide more diversity.
oneOf.title: Entries

random_seed.description: Random seed for deterministic dataset generation. When set, makes synthetic prompts, sampling, and other random operations reproducible across runs. Essential for A/B testing and debugging. Overrides global random_seed for this dataset.
random_seed.title: Random Seed

sampling.description: Strategy for selecting entries from dataset during benchmarking. sequential: iterate in order, wrapping to start after end. random: randomly sample with replacement (entries may repeat). shuffle: random permutation without replacement, re-shuffling after exhaustion.

prompts.description: Prompt/token length configuration specifying ISL, OSL, sequence distributions, and batch processing settings.

prefix_prompts.description: Shared prefix configuration for KV cache testing. Generates prefix prompts that are prepended to user prompts, simulating cached context scenarios.

turns.description: Number of request-response turns per conversation. Can be a fixed integer or {mean, stddev} distribution. Each turn consists of a user message and model response. Set to 1 for single-turn interactions. Multi-turn conversations enable testing of context retention and conversation history handling.
turns.title: Turns

turn_delay.description: Delay in milliseconds between consecutive turns within a multi-turn conversation. Can be a fixed value or {mean, stddev} distribution. Simulates user think time between receiving a response and sending the next message. Only applies when turns > 1. Set to 0 for back-to-back turns.
turn_delay.title: Turn Delay

turn_delay_ratio.description: Multiplier for scaling all turn delays. Applied after mean/stddev calculation: actual_delay = calculated_delay * ratio. Values < 1 speed up conversations, > 1 slow them down. Set to 0 to eliminate delays entirely.
turn_delay_ratio.title: Turn Delay Ratio

oneOf.description: Multiplier for scaling all turn delays. Applied after mean/stddev calculation: actual_delay = calculated_delay * ratio. Values < 1 speed up conversations, > 1 slow them down. Set to 0 to eliminate delays entirely.
oneOf.title: Turn Delay Ratio

images.description: Synthetic image configuration for multimodal vision-language testing.

audio.description: Synthetic audio configuration for multimodal speech/audio testing.

video.description: Synthetic video configuration for multimodal video understanding testing.

rankings.description: Rankings/reranking configuration for generating query-passage pairs. Only relevant for rankings endpoint types.

SyntheticDataset.title: SyntheticDataset

TcpCommunicationConfig.description:
TCP socket communication configuration.

For distributed deployments across machines.
Provides detailed port configuration for all ZMQ proxies.

host.description: Host address for TCP communication. Use 0.0.0.0 to listen on all interfaces.
host.title: Host

TcpCommunicationConfig.title: TcpCommunicationConfig

TcpProxyConfig.description: TCP proxy port configuration for a single ZMQ proxy.
TcpProxyConfig.title: TcpProxyConfig

TemplateConfig.description:
Configuration for custom template-based endpoints.

When endpoint type is "template", this configures how requests
are formatted and responses are parsed.

body.description: Jinja2 template string for request body. Variables: {{prompt}}, {{max_tokens}}, {{model}}, {{messages}}, etc.
body.title: Body

response_field.description: JSON path to extract response text from API response. Use dot notation for nested fields: 'choices.0.message.content'.
response_field.title: Response Field

TemplateConfig.title: TemplateConfig

TokenizerConfig.description:
Tokenizer configuration for token counting and prompt generation.

AIPerf uses a HuggingFace tokenizer for accurate token counting,
which is essential for ISL/OSL enforcement and metrics calculation.

name.description: HuggingFace tokenizer identifier or local filesystem path. Should match the model's tokenizer for accurate token counts. Example: 'meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct'
name.title: Name

revision.description: Model revision to use: branch name, tag, or commit hash. Use for version pinning to ensure reproducibility.
revision.title: Revision

trust_remote_code.description: Allow execution of custom tokenizer code from the repository. Required for some models but poses security risk. Only enable for trusted sources.
trust_remote_code.title: Trust Remote Code

TokenizerConfig.title: TokenizerConfig

TokenizerOverride.description:
Per-model tokenizer override configuration.

Allows specifying a different tokenizer for a specific model,
useful when models require specialized tokenization.

name.description: HuggingFace tokenizer identifier or local filesystem path.
name.title: Name

TokenizerOverride.title: TokenizerOverride

UserCentricPhase.description:
N concurrent users sharing a global request rate.

Requires multi-turn conversations. Each user gets a proportional
share of the global ``rate``.

type.description: N users sharing a global request rate.
type.title: Type

dataset.description: Name of dataset to use (from datasets section). If not specified, uses first defined dataset.
dataset.title: Dataset

exclude_from_results.description: Exclude this phase's metrics from final results. Set to true for warmup or cooldown phases.
exclude_from_results.title: Exclude From Results

anyOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'requests'.

requests.description: Stop after this many requests sent (must be >= 1).
requests.title: Requests

duration.description: Stop after this time elapsed (must be > 0). Supports: 300, '5m', '2h'. Accepts number (seconds) or string like '30s', '5m', '2h'.
duration.title: Duration

sessions.description: Stop after this many sessions completed (must be >= 1).
sessions.title: Sessions

anyOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'concurrency'.

concurrency.description: Max concurrent in-flight requests (must be >= 1). For concurrency type: primary control. For rate types: acts as a cap.
concurrency.title: Concurrency

concurrency_ramp.description: Ramp concurrency from lower value. Can be number (seconds) or {duration, strategy}.

prefill_concurrency.description: Max concurrent requests in prefill stage (must be >= 1). Limits requests before first token received.
prefill_concurrency.title: Prefill Concurrency

prefill_ramp.description: Ramp prefill_concurrency from lower value. Can be number (seconds) or {duration, strategy}.

grace_period.description: Seconds to wait for in-flight requests after duration expires (must be >= 0). Requires 'duration' to be set. Supports: 30, '30s', '2m'. Accepts number (seconds) or string like '30s', '5m', '2h'.
grace_period.title: Grace Period

seamless.description: Start this phase immediately when previous phase stops, without waiting for in-flight requests to complete. Cannot be True for the first phase.
seamless.title: Seamless

rate.description: Target request rate in requests per second (must be > 0).
rate.title: Rate

oneOf.description: Target request rate in requests per second (must be > 0).
oneOf.title: Rate

oneOf.description: List of values to sweep over for 'rate'.

rate_ramp.description: Ramp rate from lower value. Can be number (seconds) or {duration, strategy}.

users.description: Number of simulated concurrent users (must be >= 1). Requests distributed across users to achieve global rate.
users.title: Users

oneOf.description: Number of simulated concurrent users (must be >= 1). Requests distributed across users to achieve global rate.
oneOf.title: Users

UserCentricPhase.title: UserCentricPhase

VideoAudioCodec.description: Audio codecs for embedding audio in synthetic video files.
VideoAudioCodec.title: VideoAudioCodec

VideoAudioConfig.description: Configuration for embedding an audio track in synthetic video files.

sample_rate.description: Audio sample rate in Hz for the embedded audio track. Common values: 8000 (telephony), 16000 (speech), 44100 (CD quality), 48000 (professional). Higher sample rates increase audio fidelity and file size.
sample_rate.title: Sample Rate

oneOf.description: Audio sample rate in Hz for the embedded audio track. Common values: 8000 (telephony), 16000 (speech), 44100 (CD quality), 48000 (professional). Higher sample rates increase audio fidelity and file size.
oneOf.title: Sample Rate

channels.description: Number of audio channels to embed in generated video files. 0 = disabled (no audio track, default), 1 = mono, 2 = stereo. When set to 1 or 2, a Gaussian noise audio track matching the video duration is muxed into each video via FFmpeg.
channels.title: Channels

oneOf.description: Number of audio channels to embed in generated video files. 0 = disabled (no audio track, default), 1 = mono, 2 = stereo. When set to 1 or 2, a Gaussian noise audio track matching the video duration is muxed into each video via FFmpeg.
oneOf.title: Channels

codec.description: Audio codec for the embedded audio track. If not specified, auto-selects based on video format: aac for MP4, libvorbis for WebM. Options: aac, libvorbis, libopus.

depth.description: Audio bit depth for the embedded audio track. Supported values: 8, 16, 24, or 32 bits. Higher bit depths provide greater dynamic range but increase file size.
depth.title: Depth

oneOf.description: Audio bit depth for the embedded audio track. Supported values: 8, 16, 24, or 32 bits. Higher bit depths provide greater dynamic range but increase file size.
oneOf.title: Depth

VideoAudioConfig.title: VideoAudioConfig

VideoConfig.description:
Configuration for synthetic video generation in multimodal datasets.

Controls the generation of synthetic videos for video understanding
model benchmarking. Requires FFmpeg for video generation.

batch_size.description: Number of video files to include in each multimodal request. Supported with chat endpoint type for video understanding models. Set to 0 to disable video inputs. Higher batch sizes significantly increase request payload size.
batch_size.title: Batch Size

oneOf.description: Number of video files to include in each multimodal request. Supported with chat endpoint type for video understanding models. Set to 0 to disable video inputs. Higher batch sizes significantly increase request payload size.
oneOf.title: Batch Size

duration.description: Duration in seconds for each generated video clip. Combined with fps, determines total frame count (frames = duration * fps). Longer durations increase file size and processing time. Typical values: 1-10 seconds for testing.
duration.title: Duration

oneOf.description: Duration in seconds for each generated video clip. Combined with fps, determines total frame count (frames = duration * fps). Longer durations increase file size and processing time. Typical values: 1-10 seconds for testing.
oneOf.title: Duration

fps.description: Frames per second for generated video. Higher FPS creates smoother video but increases frame count and file size. Common values: 4 (minimal, recommended for Cosmos models), 24 (cinematic), 30 (standard), 60 (high frame rate). Total frames = duration * fps.
fps.title: Fps

oneOf.description: Frames per second for generated video. Higher FPS creates smoother video but increases frame count and file size. Common values: 4 (minimal, recommended for Cosmos models), 24 (cinematic), 30 (standard), 60 (high frame rate). Total frames = duration * fps.
oneOf.title: Fps

width.description: Video frame width in pixels. Determines video resolution and file size. Common values: 640 (SD), 1280 (HD), 1920 (Full HD). If not specified, uses codec/format defaults.
width.title: Width

height.description: Video frame height in pixels. Combined with width determines aspect ratio and total pixel count per frame. Common values: 480 (SD), 720 (HD), 1080 (Full HD). If not specified, uses codec/format defaults.
height.title: Height

format.description: Container format for generated video files. webm: VP9 codec, BSD-licensed, recommended for open-source workflows. mp4: H.264/H.265, widely compatible. avi: legacy, larger files. mkv: Matroska, flexible container. Format affects compatibility, file size, and encoding options.

codec.description: Video codec for encoding. Common options: libvpx-vp9 (CPU, BSD-licensed, default for WebM), libx264 (CPU, GPL, widely compatible), libx265 (CPU, GPL, smaller files), h264_nvenc (NVIDIA GPU), hevc_nvenc (NVIDIA GPU, smaller files). Any FFmpeg-supported codec can be used.
codec.title: Codec

synth_type.description: Algorithm for generating synthetic video content. Different types produce different visual patterns for testing. Content doesn't affect semantic meaning but may impact encoding efficiency and file size.

audio.description: Audio track configuration for embedding audio in generated videos.

VideoConfig.title: VideoConfig

VideoFormat.description: Video container formats for synthetic video generation.
VideoFormat.title: VideoFormat

description: JSON Schema for AIPerf YAML configuration files. This schema enables IDE autocompletion and validation for AIPerf benchmark configurations. Schema version: 2.0.0

models.description: Model configuration. Accepts a single model name string, a list of model names, or an advanced configuration with strategy and weighted items. All forms are normalized to ModelsAdvanced. Accepts a model name string, list of model names, or full ModelsAdvanced config.

oneOf.description: List of model names (normalized to ModelsAdvanced).

endpoint.description: Endpoint configuration for connecting to inference server(s). Includes URLs, API type, authentication, timeout, and connection settings.

datasets.description: Named dataset configurations. Keys are dataset names that can be referenced in phases. Values are dataset configs (synthetic, file, public, or composed with source+augment).
datasets.title: Datasets

phases.description: Benchmark phase configuration. Can be a single phase config (with 'type' key) or named phases (dict of phase configs). Single config is normalized to {'default': config}. Order is preserved (Python 3.7+) for execution sequence.
phases.title: Phases

artifacts.description: Artifacts configuration for benchmark output. Controls output directory and export formats.

slos.description: SLO (Service Level Objectives) configuration as a generic dict. Maps metric names to threshold values. A request is counted as good only if it meets ALL specified thresholds.
slos.title: Slos

tokenizer.description: Tokenizer configuration for token counting. Used for ISL/OSL enforcement and accurate metrics. If not specified, uses the first model name.

gpu_telemetry.description: GPU telemetry configuration for DCGM metrics collection. Collects GPU metrics (power, utilization, temperature) from DCGM endpoints. Enabled by default. Set enabled: false to disable.

server_metrics.description: Server metrics configuration for Prometheus scraping. Collects operational metrics (queue depth, KV cache, batch sizes) from inference server Prometheus endpoints. Enabled by default. Set enabled: false to disable.

runtime.description: Runtime configuration for worker processes and inter-process communication.

accuracy.description: Accuracy benchmarking configuration. When set, enables accuracy evaluation alongside performance profiling.

random_seed.description: Global random seed for reproducibility. Can be overridden per-dataset. If not set, uses system entropy.
random_seed.title: Random Seed

sweep.description: Sweep configuration for parameter exploration. Supports grid (Cartesian product), scenarios (hand-picked), and sequential (ordered) sweep strategies.
sweep.title: Sweep

multi_run.description: Multi-run benchmarking configuration for statistical reporting. When num_runs > 1, executes multiple runs and computes aggregate statistics.

model.description: Shorthand for 'models'. Accepts a single value which will be converted to a list/dict. Model configuration. Accepts a single model name string, a list of model names, or an advanced configuration with strategy and weighted items. All forms are normalized to ModelsAdvanced.

oneOf.description: Model configuration. Accepts a single model name string, a list of model names, or an advanced configuration with strategy and weighted items. All forms are normalized to ModelsAdvanced.

dataset.description: Shorthand for 'datasets'. Accepts a single value which will be converted to a list/dict. Named dataset configurations. Keys are dataset names that can be referenced in phases. Values are dataset configs (synthetic, file, public, or composed with source+augment).

oneOf.description: Named dataset configurations. Keys are dataset names that can be referenced in phases. Values are dataset configs (synthetic, file, public, or composed with source+augment).
oneOf.title: Datasets

title: AIPerf Configuration Schema
$comment: Auto-generated by tools/generate_config_schema.py. Do not edit manually.
